$schema: Over Your Shoulder — editorial copy library
$description: All dynamic copy for the report card. To add, remove, or edit punchlines, simply modify this file. The report reads this at load time and picks one entry per zone at random. Future languages can be added as sibling keys under each zone (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'de').

en: Your browser had a chattier day than you.
Silent to you. Loud to Silicon Valley.
The quietest thing in this laptop is you.
Your keyboard rested. Your data didn't.
You closed the lid. It kept whispering.
Your data has more hobbies than you.
You think it was quiet. It wasn't.
Every prompt has a foreign address.
AI doesn't sleep. Your browser doesn't either.
Your browser has friends you don't.

mapHeadline.$note: Contextual variants keyed by the dominant destination country (>=70% of traffic) and whether the user is IN that country (domestic) or OUTSIDE it (dominantByCountry). If no country crosses the 70% threshold, the neutral pool is used. {N} resolves to the count for the dominant country; in the neutral pool {N} is the grand total and {C} is the number of distinct destination countries.

US: Your data barely left the state today. {N} trips.
Silicon Valley stayed in the loop. {N} updates.
{N} domestic transmissions. All staying on American soil.
Your browser didn't leave the country, but it never stopped talking.
CN: Your data stayed inside the Great Firewall today. {N} trips.
{N} conversations, all within the country's borders.
FR: Your data stayed French today — {N} times.
{N} round trips that never left French territory.
GB: Your data stayed home today. {N} local trips.
Domestic traffic only. {N} conversations on British soil.
DE: Frankfurt to Munich and back — {N} domestic trips.
Your data respected its borders today. {N} internal requests.
CH: Your data stayed in the Alps today. {N} trips.
A rare day — all {N} conversations stayed local.

US: Your data crossed the Atlantic {N} times today.
Your browser phoned California {N} times today.
{N} transmissions left this laptop for the US today.
{N} postcards to America. Return address: you.
Silicon Valley received {N} updates about you today.
Today, your browser took {N} meetings abroad.
CN: Your data made {N} trips to Chinese servers today.
{N} messages for a Chinese inbox. Yours.
Shenzhen and Hangzhou got {N} reports about your day.
FR: Your data visited France {N} times today.
{N} round trips between your browser and Paris.
A French server heard from you {N} times today.
GB: London got {N} updates about what you're doing.
DE: Frankfurt's data centers heard from you {N} times.
{N} requests landed on German servers today.
Your browser spent {N} visits in Germany.

neutral: {N} outbound calls from a supposedly private browser.
Your browser reached {C} countries today. {N} times.

$tokens.{N}: domestic: count for user's own country; dominantByCountry: count for the dominant destination country; neutral: total requests across all countries.
$tokens.{C}: number of distinct destination countries (neutral pool only)

verdict.$note: Displayed next to the score ring. Deterministic by score band, random within the band. The band thresholds must mirror lib/score.js.

$howToAddPunchlines: Add a new string to any zone's array. The report picks one at random on each open. Keep masthead lines under 48 characters to fit in the single-line header. For mapHeadline, always include either {N} or both {N} and {C} tokens. For verdict, ensure the line fits in ~30 characters. No HTML, no line breaks.
$howToAddLanguage: To add French, duplicate the 'en' key to 'fr' at each zone and translate. Then add a language toggle in the UI that sets a preference in chrome.storage.local. The loader will read that preference and pick the right sub-array.
